mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing required returns and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.2% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.2%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth that lifts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion indicate supply-side bottlenecks and capacity constraints that compress profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative returns fail to materialize in late-cycle periods, or the signal shows no directional bias (Rank IC near zero) when filtered by late-cycle macro conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.0% imposes higher borrowing costs that suppress corporate investment and equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity that stimulate downstream economic output, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in over 55% of housing start expansion periods, or the series shows no persistent predictive relationship with equity returns (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 108 during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization and output acceleration that boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive returns fail to materialize in post-shock recovery periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < -0.02) when regime-filtered.
